APPEAL RECORD CERTIFIED TO THE COURT OF APPEAL (1 VOL. OF CT, NONE RT [ATTACHED TO NOA, DESIGNATION, AND AMENDED DESIGNATION] APPEAL FILED 6-13-18, A155058 DIV2)
NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AFTER ARGUMENT AT HEARING, THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF JOHN FITCH'S REQUEST THAT A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION BE ISSUED. THE COURT RULES THAT IT WOULD CAUSE GREAT DISRUPTION AND BE CONTRARY TO THE PUBLIC INTEREST TO POSTPONE THE ELECTION WHICH IS HAPPENING TOMORROW. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 (302/HK)

RESPONSE TO DEFENDANTS DEMURRER FILED BY PLAINTIFF FITCH, JOHN

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DEPARTMENT OF ELECTIONS FOR THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO THE ENTIRETY OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF JOHN FITCH IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. WITH THE ELECTION OF THE NEW MAYOR, ALL OF MR. FITCH'S CLAIMS ARE NOW MOOT. ORDER IS SIGNED.(SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: VICKI GROSS, CSR #5525, VGROSS@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Fitch, 415-678-6915 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Wayn Snodgrass, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-4700 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant DEPARTMENT OF ELECTIONS.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues to overrule demurrer. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT DEPARTMENT OF ELECTIONS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Department of Elections for the City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the entirety of the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff John Fitch is sustained without leave to amend. With the election of the new mayor, all of Mr. Fitch's claims are now moot. Even if those claims were not moot, they lack merit as a matter of law because Mr. Fitch is not entitled to be mayor.

Order is signed.(see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR #5525, vgross@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John Fitch, Tel. No. 1-415-678-6915, Plaintiff appearing in pro per;
Atty. Jon Givner, Deputy City Attorney, Tel. No. 1-415-554-4894 appearing for the
Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Be Issued is Denied, it will cause great disruption and be contrary to the public interest to postpone the election which is happening tomorrow. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
